suggestions.title: Bloberto hardcoded to never receive ping reactions
suggestions.description: Line 1543 of Office.jsx passes `pingReaction={null}` hardcoded to Bloberto's Character. The entire ping + reaction system (PING_REACTIONS map, pingReaction state, handleCharClick) supports per-worker quotes, but Bloberto is permanently excluded. Either intentional (document it clearly) or a bug — if intentional, Bloberto should have manager-specific ping reactions since he already fires ambient broadcast quotes. Fix: wire up pingReaction for Bloberto the same way other workers are wired, or add a comment explaining the design choice.

suggestions.title: activityLog.filter() called 3× inline in the same render — not memoized
suggestions.description: In App.jsx, `activityLog.filter()` is called three separate times in the dashboard render path: once for the count display (inline IIFE), once for the ActivityLog component `entries` prop, and a third time with the same logic but chained. These are not wrapped in useMemo so they re-run on every render cycle including the 60-second tick from useTimeTick. Extract into a single `useMemo(() => filteredActivity, [activityLog, activityFilter, workerFilter])` and pass the result downstream. Low effort, real gain as the activity log grows.

suggestions.title: Hover cards are completely inaccessible on touch devices
suggestions.description: Character hover cards in the Office view (the char-hover-card showing task + progress) are triggered by `onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave`. On touch devices (the whole mobile experience), these never fire. Mobile users clicking a character do get the ping animation and are navigated to the dashboard, but they can't see the tooltip/hover-card inline info. Fix: add `onTouchStart` → `setPinnedCard(true)` with a tap-outside-to-dismiss handler, or convert to a proper popover accessible on touch. The `@media (hover: none)` block in Office.css exists but only suppresses hover styling — it doesn't provide an alternative interaction.

suggestions.title: WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES used 543 lines before it's defined — temporal dead zone bomb
suggestions.description: In Office.jsx, `WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES` is referenced at line 595 (inside the Character component's useEffect) but defined at line 1138. This works at runtime because the useEffect fires after module evaluation, but it's a hoisting trap: during refactoring or if the Component is called during module init, you'd get a ReferenceError. Move all constant tables (WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES, FULL_IDLE_MESSAGES, PLANT_STAGE_MESSAGES, BLOBERTO_TITLES, VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS, DOOR_ICONS, etc.) to the top of the file, or better yet into utils/constants.js which already holds this category of data.

suggestions.title: Footer cycling quotes silently stop — no user feedback
suggestions.description: The footer's 'tap for another thought' button cycles through BONUS_TAGLINES (8 quotes). The condition `quoteBonus < BONUS_TAGLINES.length` in the counter display means nothing shows after the last quote — the button still renders but clicking it does nothing visible (the `setQuoteBonus(p => p < BONUS_TAGLINES.length ? p + 1 : p)` guard silently prevents incrementing). Users will tap a button that looks functional and get no response. Fix: either loop with modulo, show a 'all out of wisdom 🫠' state, or add a visual indicator that the well is dry.

suggestions.title: Character name labels at 0.44rem are unreadably small on mobile
suggestions.description: The `.char__name` font-size drops to 0.44rem at ≤600px breakpoint in Office.css. At 375px viewport (standard iPhone), this renders name labels as approximately 6-7px text — well below WCAG minimum of 11px for non-decorative text. The Office view already compensates by navigating to dashboard on character click, but the names are decorative enough to warrant at least 0.65rem. Consider hiding the inline name label on mobile and relying solely on the dashboard card view, or bump to 0.6–0.7rem and accept potential overlap.

suggestions.title: App.jsx and Office.jsx are monolith files (1432 and 1660 lines)
suggestions.description: App.jsx contains the ErrorBoundary, all utility functions (formatFullDateTime, getDispatch, getTimeGroup), all sub-components (ActivityLogEntry, ActivityLog, StatusBadge, ProgressBar, WorkerCard, VibeSparkline, DeltaBadge, StatsBar, EmptyState, LoadingSkeleton, ShortcutToast), all constants (STAT_DISPATCHES, TASK_TAG_DEFS, VIBE_COLORS, VIBE_RANK, etc.), and the root App component. Office.jsx has 1660 lines of the same pattern. This makes diffs noisy, code reviews painful, and onboarding slow. Recommended split: extract WorkerCard + StatusBadge + ProgressBar → components/WorkerCard.jsx, StatsBar + VibeSparkline → components/StatsBar.jsx, ActivityLog* → components/ActivityLog.jsx. In Office.jsx, extract Character + CharacterAvatar → components/Character.jsx.

suggestions.title: 103 timer instances (setTimeout/setInterval) with complex interdependencies
suggestions.description: App.jsx has 46 setTimeout/setInterval calls and Office.jsx has 57. While most have corresponding cleanups, the sheer volume makes auditing correctness difficult. Specific concern: in Character.jsx, the `idleInnerTimeoutRef` and `errorInnerTimeoutRef` are separate from `timerRef` but all three can fire near-simultaneously during status changes. The `justShippedTimersRef` array pattern for staggered confetti is correct but fragile — the cleanup in the useEffect return fires before new timers are set on the next effect run, creating a brief double-scheduling window. Consider a custom `useTimers()` hook that auto-registers all timers for unified cleanup.

suggestions.title: Task tags missing role/tabIndex when onTagClick is null
suggestions.description: In WorkerCard, task tags conditionally receive `role='button'`, `tabIndex={0}`, and keyboard handlers only when `onTagClick` is provided. When onTagClick is null (the prop defaults to null), the tags are inert spans. However, they still render inside a card with `tabIndex={0}` and `role='article'` — a keyboard user navigating the card will find seemingly clickable colored labels that do nothing and have no role. Since onTagClick appears to always be passed in the current dashboard render, this is a latent risk. Add role='button' unconditionally when tags are displayed, or document that these are display-only without the handler.

suggestions.title: completionToast and plantToast use inline styles instead of CSS classes
suggestions.description: Both toast elements (completionToast in App.jsx and plantToast in Office.jsx) are implemented as fully inline-styled divs with hardcoded position, background, border, padding, font-weight, boxShadow, zIndex, etc. This makes them impossible to override with CSS theming (light mode), hard to animate uniformly, and duplicated logic. A shared `.toast` CSS class with modifier variants would allow the light theme to properly style these. Right now in light mode, the toast border inherits `var(--surface2)` background which may be nearly invisible against the page background.

suggestions.title: Worker filter buttons in activity log will overflow on narrow mobile screens
suggestions.description: The second activity filter bar (the per-worker filter row) renders a button for every roster member inline. On a 375px screen with 5+ workers, these buttons will either overflow, wrap awkwardly, or trigger a horizontal scrollbar. The existing activity type filter bar has the same issue but has fewer items. Both need either `flex-wrap: wrap` with proper gutters, or a `<select>` dropdown for mobile. Currently there is no max-width scroll container or wrapping rule for `.activity-filter-bar` below 480px.

suggestions.title: Idle micro-bubble selection uses a complex inline Date.now() expression in render
suggestions.description: Line 786 of Office.jsx: `(WORKER_IDLE_BUBBLES[worker.id] || DEFAULT_IDLE_BUBBLES)[Math.floor((Date.now() / 12000) + (worker.id ? [...worker.id].reduce((h, c) => h * 31 + c.charCodeAt(0), 0) : 0)) % ...]` — this is a time-based deterministic selector running on every render. While clever (same worker always shows same bubble in a given 12s window), it's illegible, fragile, and causes a subtle issue: when the 12s boundary crosses during a render, the bubble can change mid-display without the `idleBubble` state changing. Extract into a `getIdleBubble(workerId, tick)` utility function and pass in a `tick` derived from `useTimeTick(12000)`.

suggestions.title: VIBE_RANK, VIBE_COLORS, VIBE_HISTORY_KEY, BLOBERTO_TITLES scattered across files
suggestions.description: Constants that describe the same domain concept (vibe states) are split between App.jsx (VIBE_RANK, VIBE_COLORS, VIBE_HISTORY_KEY, HONORIFICS), Office.jsx (BLOBERTO_TITLES, VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS, DOOR_ICONS, DOOR_LABELS), and utils/constants.js (VIBE_WHITEBOARD, ROLE_EMOJIS). There's no single source of truth for vibe-related mappings. Consolidate vibe constants into utils/vibe.js or a new utils/vibeConstants.js. This would make adding a new vibe state a single-file change instead of hunting across 3 files.

suggestions.title: Dashboard solo worker card is center-aligned but left-aligned on reload
suggestions.description: When exactly 1 worker is active, the dashboard uses a special `workers-grid--solo-card` layout that centers the card. If the page loads with 1 worker already active (no transition from 0→1), the card appears immediately centered which is fine. But when transitioning from 2+ workers to 1, the fadingMap causes the fading card to still be present and the solo layout doesn't activate (condition checks `Object.keys(fadingMap).length === 0`), so the single worker snaps to the normal left-aligned grid, then jumps to centered after the fade completes 400ms later. A smooth transition would feel more polished.

suggestions.title: No deep-linking to a specific worker card
suggestions.description: The app supports tab deep-linking via URL hash (#office, #dashboard) which is great. But there's no way to link directly to a specific worker's card (e.g., #dashboard/sofia). The focusedWorker mechanism already scrolls to and highlights a worker when navigating from office→dashboard click, so the infrastructure exists. Adding support for ?worker=sofia in the URL would allow sharing a direct link to a specific team member's status, which is genuinely useful for async check-ins.

suggestions.title: Vibe sparkline re-reads localStorage on every vibe change instead of using state
suggestions.description: In the vibe streak useEffect in App.jsx, `localStorage.getItem(VIBE_HISTORY_KEY)` and `JSON.parse` are called on every teamVibe.key change. The result is then stored in both localStorage and React state (vibeHistory). This is fine for infrequent vibe changes but it means the component is bypassing its own state (vibeHistory) to re-read from storage on every update, which is redundant. Use the vibeHistory state as the source of truth and only write to localStorage as a side effect. Also: the try/catch around the localStorage block silently swallows all errors — at minimum log them in development.

summary: Bloberto's Office is a genuinely delightful product — it's ambitious, characterful, and packed with thoughtful details that make monitoring a team actually fun. The vibe system, custom SVG characters, per-worker desk screens, ambient quotes, dynamic favicon, and plant streak are all A+ ideas executed well. That said, the codebase is starting to creak under its own weight: App.jsx and Office.jsx are monoliths at 1432 and 1660 lines respectively, 103 timer instances create a subtle memory/state correctness risk, and the activity log filter runs three inline passes per render when one memoized pass would do. On the frontend quality side, the hardcoded `pingReaction={null}` on Bloberto is a quiet bug that kills a feature that's otherwise fully implemented, and mobile touch users are locked out of the hover-card details that desktop users get on mouse-over. The 0.44rem character name labels on mobile are technically readable only by people with microscopes. Overall: the fun factor and feature set are legitimately impressive for what this is — it just needs a refactor pass to split the files, memoize the hot paths, and fix the handful of bugs before adding more features.
